stack.rationale: Trackpad haptics, a menu-bar presence, and unobtrusive native cues are the market standard for this class and require native APIs — a web/extension wrapper cannot deliver them at quality.

uxStructure: NSStatusItem menu-bar icon; click opens a compact SwiftUI popover
popover: pattern picker (Box 4-4-4-4 default), a big Start control, and a minimalist breathing animation
no main window — menu-bar only, never steals focus from the active app

errorHandling: if trackpad haptics are unavailable (no Force Touch trackpad), fall back to visual + audio cues
if audio playback fails, continue silently with visual + haptic cues
testPlan: menu-bar click then Start shows a guide animating on a 4-4-4-4 cadence
toggling sound off removes audio while visual and haptic cues continue
quitting and relaunching restores the last pattern and preferences

build.signing: ad-hoc for local run; Developer ID + notarization for distribution

decisionLog.selectionCriteria: native APIs required for trackpad haptics, menu-bar presence, and unobtrusive feel
decisionLog.rationale: only native APIs deliver the market-standard haptic/menu-bar experience
decisionLog.tracesTo: appClass + baselineRequirements (trackpad haptic cue, menu-bar presence)

decisionLog.selectionCriteria: unobtrusive focus tool that never steals focus
decisionLog.rationale: market standard for an unobtrusive focus tool
decisionLog.tracesTo: customDelta (focus during work, runs during calls)

decisionLog.selectionCriteria: a few flat preferences, no relational or large data
decisionLog.rationale: smallest mechanism that fits the data shape
visual+audio+haptic is the baseline users expect for this class

decisionLog.selectionCriteria: least privilege; high-risk items need explicit consent
decisionLog.rationale: notifications and Health are high-risk and unconfirmed; keep the base app permission-free

decisionLog.selectionCriteria: robust across Mac hardware without Force Touch
decisionLog.rationale: fall back to visual/audio when haptics are unavailable
decisionLog.tracesTo: successCriteria (cues stay in sync with the pattern)

decisionLog.rationale: no App Store constraints needed for a local utility

openConfirmations.rationale: only if the user enables reminders; touches the notifications permission
openConfirmations.tracesTo: unknowns (does the user want daily reminders?)

openConfirmations.decision: Apple Health write for mindful minutes
openConfirmations.rationale: health data; include only with explicit user consent
